x-enumeration-literals.name: Firm Commitment Underwriting
x-enumeration-literals.description:
The underwriter purchases the entire issue from the issuer and resells it to investors. The underwriter assumes full financial risk for any unsold securities.

Comment:
Example:
GlobalTech issues €500M in bonds. AlphaBank (lead underwriter) buys all €500M and resells it to investors. If some bonds remain unsold, AlphaBank bears the loss.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Best Efforts Underwriting
x-enumeration-literals.description:
The underwriter does not guarantee the full subscription. They make their best effort to sell securities but the issuer receives only what is sold.

Comment:
Example:
A start-up issues €10M in shares. BetaBank underwrites on a best-efforts basis; if only €7M is sold, the issuer receives €7M, and the underwriter has no liability for the unsold €3M.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Standby Underwriting
x-enumeration-literals.description:
The underwriter agrees to purchase any securities not subscribed by existing investors. Often used in rights issues or pre-emptive offerings.

Comment:
Example:
GlobalTech offers new shares to existing shareholders. GammaCapital stands by to buy any shares not taken up, ensuring the company raises the full amount.

x-enumeration-literals.name: All-or-None Underwriting
x-enumeration-literals.description:
All-or-None (AON): The deal proceeds only if the entire issue is fully subscribed. If minimum subscription is not reached, the issue is canceled.

Comment:
Example:
A municipal bond of €100M will only be issued if all €100M is subscribed; otherwise, the underwriting is void, and no funds are raised.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Mini-Max Underwriting
x-enumeration-literals.description:
A hybrid type where a minimum amount must be sold, and the underwriter guarantees a maximum capped amount.

comment:
Example:
An IPO with a minimum subscription of €20M and a maximum of €50M; the underwriter purchases up to €50M, but the issuer must reach at least €20M to proceed.
